codeReview.summary: The file split is substantial and mostly aligned with the feature scope, but it introduced a linux-target compile break in the core runtime file.

issues.description: buildCHInstance returns `runCtx.Err` (a method value) in a position typed as `error`. This is a compile-time type mismatch (`func() error` is not assignable to `error`) for linux builds, breaking the runtime package on its target platform.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The refactor-worker procedure did not require a linux-target compile check when editing `_linux.go` files, so host-only validation on macOS missed a linux-only compile error.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff verification only includes `go build ./...`, `go vet ./...`, and `go test ./...`; this feature changed linux-tagged files and contains a linux compile error at internal/runtime/cloud_hypervisor_linux.go:223.

sharedStateObservations.observation: .factory/services.yaml lacks a linux-target validation command for platform-specific runtime code.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml defines only host-platform build/vet/test commands; no GOOS=linux command is present despite linux-specific files under internal/runtime/.

summary: Fail: structural split goals were met, but a blocking linux-only compile error was introduced in `buildCHInstance`.
